validator_match_examples.source_requirement: Allowing users to see and change profiles: If user-specified profiles are used, users should be able to see, modify and delete that profile on demand.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: If a user performs a quick or advanced search, the System must never include in the search result list any record which the user does not have the right to access.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: Linking back to the home page or landmark pages: Each page should contain a link leading to the home page of the application or to a landmark page that is easy to recognize for the user.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: Using familiar terminology for navigation links: Navigation links — particularly links representing the main navigation structure — should be labelled with terms that are familiar to the user, based on his/her general knowledge, prior experience in the application domain or experience of using other systems.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: REGISTRY SERVICES ENVIRONMENT REQUIREMENTS The Registry software shall be portable and required to execute on COE-compliant platforms.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The software should be able to run on multiple platforms without significant modifications.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement captures portability but misses the critical detail of 'COE-compliant platforms' and weakens the modality from 'shall' to 'should'.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: The Qualification Methods applied to the software shall include test, demonstration, analysis, and inspection (T, D, A, I).
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The software must support automated testing across all environments to catch issues early.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement only covers 'test' from the source and adds the detail of 'automated testing across all environments' and a purpose, while omitting 'demonstration, analysis, and inspection'.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: End User Mode: Portion of XS services shall be used by various classes of users: data consumers, data and database managers, network information infrastructure resource managers.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The software should handle different types of user roles and permissions without conflicts.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement correctly identifies the need for handling different user types but generalizes the specific user classes mentioned in the source and adds a condition ('without conflicts').

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: COE sponsors shall adhere to compliance level requirements described in the I&RTS.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The software must ensure data encryption both in transit and at rest.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement introduces a specific technical solution (data encryption) and applies it to software, whereas the source is a general compliance adherence requirement for COE sponsors referencing an external document. It loses critical details about the actor and the source of compliance.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: The data management services shall support managing various types of data, database architectures and platforms that includes hardware and software at the specified sites.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The software should handle different environments, including various browsers.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement narrows the scope of 'various types of data, database architectures and platforms' to 'different environments, including various browsers,' losing critical details about data, database architectures, platforms, hardware, software, and sites. While 'environments' is related, 'browsers' is a specific and limited interpretation.

unmatched_source_examples: DOCUMENT OVERVIEW This document outlines the software capabilities required for the XS components for the DII COE.
The XS shall have the ability to redefine or reset names of connect descriptors to database server instances.
At a minimum, the session time-out values shall be user definable and be able to be reset prior to initialization of a user session.
Some of these uses of the data management services will entail unique requirements that shall be fulfilled within the capability of XS services.
In support of training activities, the data management services shall provide for the same processing as would be encountered in a production environment.
DATA ACCESS SERVICES INTERNAL INTERFACE REQUIREMENTS The XS function shall provide standard APIs as specified in Appendix C.
The XS shall internally interface (transparent to the operator) with the existing data elements of the DBIF, DAC, and COTS RDBMS products.
Personnel requirements shall be determined by the developers of the system in which the XS module is embedded TRAINING-RELATED REQUIREMENTS Not applicable.
Training requirements shall be determined by the developers of the system in which the XS module is embedded.
OTHER REQUIREMENTS None PACKAGING REQUIREMENTS The XS software shall be delivered in accordance with DII COE guidelines.

unmatched_generated_examples: The system should handle up to 1000 concurrent users without issues.
The system should be designed with clear architecture and modular components.
The system should validate credit card information for returning users without requiring re-entry.
The system should be easily ported to other platforms if needed.
The system should be designed with a robust logging mechanism for debugging and auditing purposes.

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: In the fixed (static) mode of operation (base or data processing megacenter), the data management services shall have the capability of being tuned by on-site personnel to adjust for varying workloads and sizes of associated databases.
partial_only_examples.generated_requirement: The system should handle up to 1000 concurrent users without issues.
partial_only_examples.reason: The generated requirement introduces a specific number of concurrent users, which is a material addition and a different metric than "varying workloads and sizes of associated databases". It also loses all other critical details from the source.

partial_only_examples.generated_requirement: The system should be able to receive and process XML registry forms.
partial_only_examples.reason: The generated requirement covers XML processing but misses the API provision and specific view extraction/disassembly details from the source, and adds 'registry forms'.

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: REGISTRY SERVICES ENVIRONMENT REQUIREMENTS The Registry software shall be portable and required to execute on COE-compliant platforms.
partial_only_examples.generated_requirement: The system should be easily ported to other platforms if needed.
partial_only_examples.reason: The generated requirement captures the portability aspect but weakens the modality from "shall be required" to "should be easily ported" and generalizes "COE-compliant platforms" to "other platforms", losing a specific constraint.

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: REGISTRY SERVICES ENVIRONMENT REQUIREMENTS The Registry software shall be portable and required to execute on COE-compliant platforms.

partial_only_examples.generated_requirement: The system should be able to receive and process XML registry forms.

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: But if they were identified as attributes of the element ctry, then the additional baggage (other attributes) must be carried, or submitted as separate elements.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: Each voucher slip should contain the bar code of the Voucher with two identifications one for the client and the other for the partner.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: Each voucher slip should contain a barcode with two unique identifiers: one for the client and another for the partner.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement faithfully covers the source, preserving all critical details about the barcode and its two identifiers for client and partner.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: The authorized user can alter / amend the Claim if required.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: Authorized users should be able to amend or alter claim entries if needed.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement faithfully covers the source, preserving the core need for authorized users to amend claims.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: Invoice amount = No of vouchers x Wholesale price Mode of payment is Cash The mandatory information required during a distribution transaction is mentioned below.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The system should validate invoice amounts as the number of vouchers multiplied by the wholesale price.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement faithfully captures the calculation for the invoice amount as stated in the source, interpreting it as a validation rule.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: Name of the distributor* Name of the Sales Man* Date of distribution* No of vouchers sold* According to the number of vouchers required by the distributor, the system should allocate the relevant vouchers with their numbers and batch numbers based on the stock.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: When allocating vouchers, the system must consider the distributor's name and the sales man involved, along with the date of distribution. Each voucher allocated should include its number and batch number.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement loses critical details regarding the number of vouchers sold/required by the distributor and the condition of allocation being "based on the stock". It also weakens the modality for some aspects from "should allocate" to "must consider".

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: Each voucher should have the following properties, which will have sub-elements to get the batch numbers, voucher numbers, and the project codes.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The system should include the number and batch number of each voucher allocated.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement covers voucher numbers and batch numbers but omits the critical detail of 'project codes' and 'sub-elements' mentioned in the source.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: The system should have a provision to capture the reason suppose if the claim status is “Rejection or Quarantine” Claim Amount – The calculation of claim amount is explained below.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: If the claim status is either ‘Rejection’ or ‘Quarantine’, we have a provision to capture the reason for the rejection or quarantine. This will help us understand the issues better and improve our processes.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement fully covers the part of the source about capturing the reason for claim rejection or quarantine, preserving all critical details. However, it omits the second part of the source regarding 'Claim Amount' calculation.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: During the claim entry the system should capture the following information.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: During the claim entry process, we need to capture detailed information such as the claimant's name, contact details, date of birth, and a description of the incident. This is important because it helps us verify the identity and ensure accurate claims processing.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement correctly identifies the core need of capturing information during claim entry, but it materially adds specific examples of information to be captured and a justification, which were not present in the source text. The source only stated "the following information" without specifying what.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: The Salesman master should capture following information’s.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The Salesman master should record customer's name and contact details during a sale.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement specifies particular information to be captured, which is an addition to the very general source requirement that only states "capture following information’s" without specifying what.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: In the transaction part no of forms should match with No of Available Forms The system should print a receipt document based on the information entered during claim submission.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The system should match the number of forms entered during claim submission with available forms in stock.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement covers the matching of forms but completely misses the requirement to print a receipt document.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: While entering the No of vouchers required, the system should automatically pick the Batch No’s with voucher No’s from the available voucher stock and list the details of each voucher with below information’s as a grid format.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The system should allocate relevant vouchers based on the number required by the distributor.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement covers the core need of allocating vouchers based on quantity but loses critical details about automatically picking from stock, including batch/voucher numbers, and displaying details in a grid format.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: The system admin only can able to create User Groups The system should have the provision to create any number of individual user under any user group.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: System admins should be able to add new salesmen to the Team Master.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement generalizes 'User Groups' and 'individual user' to 'Team Master' and 'salesmen', losing specific details about user management and adding new specific details.

unmatched_source_examples: The principles of the voucher, the contents of the health care services, their required quality, the standards to be met by the providers and their infrastructure, as well as the measures for monitoring the quality have been defined in the PDS.
Each voucher should have two portions with three tear off voucher slips each for Client and Partner.

This validity date can be amended or altered at the system level ONLY by the authorized user Voucher will also have MRP (maximum retail price) Voucher should have three tear off portioned slips with a sub-section tear-off for the partner.
During every distribution transaction the user should select the name of the sales man listed from Team Master.
The values of Health Sub – District, Locality, Level Of Facility, Type of facility, Registration Body would be list from their own master and should select the details based on the VSP.
The VSP Master Information screen should have a provision to enter the payment terms agreed between MSIU and VSP.

VSP Name Staff Code Staff Name Staff Type Qualification The system will generate message while creating a new staff with existing name, but the system will allow the user to save that new staff if its required.
Claim (Treatment Form) Submission Depends on the payment terms (Bi-monthly / Monthly) mentioned in the VSP master the VSP should submit the Claim (Treatment form) to the MISU Field office.
These information is vital and shall be used for compared with the processed claims.
The document should have the following information.
Before the claim entry the user should check the form manually to understand whether any mandatory information is missed in the Claim or not.
If yes, then the user should mark that Claim (Treatment Form) status as Rejected and send back to VSP.

Patient Status - (Select from list of status options populating from Patient Status Master) suppose the status is Referral then the system will have the provision to capture Referred To and Reason for Referral, the reason for Referral should be populated from Referral master.

If the claim from same VSP having more than two times of Thump Impression mismatching, then the system should automatically produce a warning message and the same time the system should In-Activate the VSP.
